summary: Backend (20/20 pytest) and Frontend E2E validation of Mahindra University Campus Hub — all features pass. Critical multi-punch toggle behavior verified end-to-end: single toggle button correctly flips between action-in (GREEN 'Check In') and action-out (CRIMSON 'Check Out') based on last accepted event today, derived from /api/attendance/history. Three successive punches (out → in → out → in) all completed via the modal flow (snap-btn → result-done) and the hero badge correctly updated to '8 PUNCHES' with 'Re-punch any time · 8 today' and subtitle 'Re-punch · Welcome back'. Admin /admin HR portal loaded cleanly with Dashboard/Attendance/Employees/Geofences/Reports tabs. Claymorphism UI (crimson + gold on warm cream) renders correctly on Login, Home, and Attendance screens. Login one-tap demo cards work. All 8 demo accounts authenticate. /api/auth/me, /api/attendance/{check,today,history,stats,geofences}, /api/admin/dashboard (admin-only, 403 for staff), /api/mess/list all return expected payloads with no MongoDB _id leakage.

issues: On 390x844 mobile viewport the modal is taller than the screen and the snap-btn renders below the fold. The modal is scrollable so it works, but consider reducing demo-avatar height or making the action bar sticky-bottom so users don't have to scroll to capture.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings: `shadow*` style props are deprecated — should migrate to `boxShadow`. Affects clay/shadow tokens in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts (and consumers in attendance.tsx, login.tsx, Clay.tsx).
Console warnings: `props.pointerEvents` is deprecated — should move pointerEvents into style. Affects shared UI primitives (likely src/ui.tsx / Clay.tsx).

Console warning: `useNativeDriver` is not supported on web because the native animated module is missing. Triggered when opening Capture modal. Either gate useNativeDriver behind Platform.OS !== 'web' or accept the JS fallback silently.

action_items: Optional polish: replace `shadow*` props with `boxShadow` and move `pointerEvents` into style — eliminates RN-Web console warnings (not blocking).
Optional polish: gate Animated useNativeDriver=true behind Platform.OS !== 'web' to silence the JS-fallback warning.
Optional UX: make CaptureFlow modal action bar (snap-btn) sticky at the bottom or shrink the demo avatar so capture button is visible without scrolling on 375–390px viewports.
critical_code_review_comments: lastEventToday derivation in attendance.tsx (lines 78-90) correctly filters by `accepted` flag and sorts desc by timestamp — matches backend behavior verified in test_lastEventToday_logic_matches_frontend.
Backend /api/attendance/today (server.py L662-687) correctly picks first accepted 'in' and last accepted 'out', enabling multi-punch UX while preserving classic check_in/check_out semantics.
/app/backend/server.py is 1388 lines — exceeds 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into modules (auth.py, attendance.py, admin.py, mess.py, etc.). Non-blocking but recommended for maintainability.
/app/frontend/app/modules/attendance.tsx is 1450 lines — should be split (TodayTab, HistoryTab, StatsTab, TeamTab, CaptureFlow each into their own files).
All endpoints exclude MongoDB `_id` via projection `{_id: 0}` or `rec.pop('_id', None)` — verified no _id leakage in responses.
seed_data_creation: Did not create new seed users. Faculty user (faculty@mahindrauniversity.edu.in) accrued 8 attendance punches during testing (3 from pytest multi-punch + 3 from playwright + 2 pre-existing today). Multi-punch logic correctly handled this state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_attendance_e2e.py is reusable — run with `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://campus-hub-453.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_attendance_e2e.py -v`. The multi-punch class accumulates punches in the live DB; if you need an idempotent reset, POST /api/admin/seed/reset-attendance as admin first. For UI testing, the Capture modal snap-btn is below the fold on 390x844 viewport — use JS click (el.scrollIntoView + el.click()) instead of page.click() to bypass viewport check.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no failing tests. Initial Playwright failure was a test-script issue (snap-btn outside viewport on small mobile screens); resolved by switching to JS-driven scrollIntoView+click. Application code is correct.
